rationale: Both correctly land on the actual resolution (close/won't-implement, attribution already covered by #3576's stage/replica relabeling, tail_waste/fragmentation withdrawn per Ronnie-Rui's own concession), but Y captures the full scope discussion (LLM_AR/LLM_Generation now, diffusion deferred to Q3) and credits both maintainers' actual arguments more completely. X introduces an unverified, oddly specific rebuttal to the make_stats throttle ('per-scheduler not global', a named regression test) that isn't established anywhere in the thread and reads as fabricated, which hurts grounding and risks a confidently-wrong claim on a point the thread never actually resolved. Y instead treats the throttle as a genuinely open, separate finding and recommends filing a focused follow-up — better calibration on an uncertain point plus stronger end-to-end completeness.
